calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Jul-20-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Pamela Cooke, via ZOOM, for Carolyn Hill, Trustee; Endy Ukoha-Ajike, via ZOOM, for Barbara Hill, Objector.

The court begins by querying counsel regarding settlement discussions and whether the Accounting Petition and Petition for Order Compelling Successor Trustee's Accounting can be taken off calendar if the court granted the Petition for Order Confirming Transfer of Assets to Trust. Ms. Cooke reports that yes, there has been settlement of the accounting petitions and the only remaining issue is the petition for order confirming transfer of assets. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike confirms Ms. Cooke's testimony.

After additional discussion, the First Account Current and Report of Successor Trustee and, Petition for Order Compelling Successor Trustee's Accounting are taken off calendar. The Petition for Order Confirming Transfer of Assets to Trust is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Joint Status Report By Parties Barbara Hill And Carolyn Hill
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Pamela Cooke appeared by zoom for Carolyn Hill (Trustee).
Endy Ukoha-Ajike appeared by zoom for Barbara Hill (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that at the next calling of this matter the Petition for Order Compelling Successor Trustee to Account filed on January 18, 2018 needs to be returned to the calendar. The Court also informed Counsel that it does not have a proposed order for the first account and report. Ms. Cooke stated that the parties participated in mediation to settle all of the petitions, that part of the settlement agreement is to fully prepare the 850 petition in order to make distribution and that all of the petitions are resolved. Ms. Cooke also stated that they now have a notice issue regarding the 850 petition and that she does not believe they need Court approval of the settle agreement. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that they do not Court approval of the settlement, and that Carolyn and Barbara are Mattie's only heirs at law. Ms. Cooke stated that they have located some of Ules' heirs, and that they need time to complete their search and provide 30-day notice. The Petition for Approval of Joint Status Report is under submission. The First Account Current and Report of Successor Trustee and the Petition for Order Confirming Transfer of Assets to Trust are continued to May 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Order Compelling Successor Trustee to Account will be added back to the calendar for hearing date of May 11, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike appeared by telephone for Barbara Hill (Objector).

Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that they tried to file a stipulation last Monday, but it got rejected, that they have met and conferred, and that they agreed to mediate on September 22, 2020. The Frist Account Current and Report of Successor Trustee is continued to October 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. A joint status report is to be filed by October 19, 2020.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Substitution Of Attorney For Successor Trustee, Caroline Hill
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To Mar-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pamela Leggett-Cooke for and with Caroline Hill.
Vernon Goins for and with Barbara Hill.

Mr. Goins stated that the respondent is avoiding the accounting, that there has been no accounting during the 8 year period, that there has been nothing distributed since the surviving spouse died almost 8 years ago, that there are 2 properties as well as several bank accounts, that this is Respondent's 3rd attorney and each time there is a new attorney they claim a change of guard but nothing has changed. Mr. Goins further stated that the Respondent does not account for rent, that the Baker property is being wasted, that there has not been increases in the rent, that his client is in the dark about rental income and expenses, that the Respondent does not take care of the water leak at the Baker property, that Respondent's daughter occupies the Colby property with no rent, and that there is a delay in resolving settlement. In addition, Mr. Goins stated that the properties are identified in the Trust but not in the Trust and the properties should be Heggstad, that this Trust needs to be administered, that Ms. Leggett-Cooke received $10,000.00 to come up with accounting by the previous attorney, that there is a question of Ms. Leggett-Cooke's ability to serve without conflict, and requested the court to suspend the Trustee and allow his client to take over. The Court stated that Mr. Goins' relief sought are not in a petition. Mr. Goins stated that he can file a petition and requested the court at minimum to suspend the Trustee for her failure to act. Ms. Leggett-Cooke stated that she is the new attorney, that she understands some of the history of this case, that she was contacted by the attorney in 2017 to prepare the accounting, that she was in the progress of preparing the accounting with all bank statements when parties were working on a settlement so the accounting was off the table, that when settlement broke down the accounting came back, that there has been cash distributions of $50,000.00 to Mr. Goins' client and attorney's fees for Mr. Goins, that the family didn't want to rent out the Colby property, that Barbara's son first occupied the Colby property and now Caroline's daughter occupies it so that property was occupied 50/50. Ms. Leggett-Cooke further stated that she has the 2 years ready to E-file and she will send the substitution of attorney to Mr. Dobrov to sign. Ms. Barbara Hill spoke of family dynamics. The Court noted that a beneficiary can make a trustee's life difficult, that solutions the court may come up with will not work for everyone, and parties can work out solutions that are appropriate.
The Court ordered Mr. Goins to file a joint status report with courtesy copies delivered 1 week before the next hearing date. The Court further noted that in the future, sanctions will be imposed, that parties need to do their parts, and if Mr. Goins is seeking other relief he needs to put it on a petition, and that the matter is on for accounting issues today.
The status of successor trustee's accounting is continued to March 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of substitution of attorney for successor trustee, Caroline Hill, is set for March 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Continued To Mar-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins appeared by telephone for Barbara Hill (Petitioner).
Michael Dobrov former Counsel for Carolyn Hill.
Pamela Cooke for and with Carolyn Hill.

The Court questions whether Ms. Cooke will be filing a substitution of attorney. Mr. Dobrov stated that nothing has been on Carolyn's behalf. The Court noted that it received a declaration from Mr. Goins on Friday and questions why a joint report wasn't filed. Mr. Goins stated that he did not send it to Mr. Dobrov, and that at the last hearing it was represented that they were resolving the matter, but they couldn't agree as to what was in the report. Mr. Dobrov stated that he was unable to do the joint status report, that he was not in communication with his Client, that he put together a joint tenants in common deed, that Ms. Cooke is putting together an accounting and that the accounting was such that he did not think he could provide an accounting to the Court that it would approve. Mr. Dobrov also stated that Mr. Goins' Client removed the request for the accountings so that they could settle and that he could not do the joint status report because they did not 100% settle the matter. Mr. Goins stated that he would like Carolyn to be removed. The Court informed Mr. Goins that he will need to file a separate petition seeking removal and suggested that Mr. Goins communicate with Ms. Cooke. Ms. Cooke stated that she is happy to complete the accounting and if it's not an issue, she is happy to continue with settlement. Status on filing of the Trustee's accounting is continued to March 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to discuss whether they will go forward with the accounting, or continue on with settlement. The Attorneys and their Clients are ordered to make personal appearances on March 9th.

Judge: Cecilia P. Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Continued To Jan-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kellyann Estrem appeared by telephone for Barbara Hill (Petitioner).
Michael Dobrov for Carolyn Hill (Respondent).

Mr. Dobrov apologized for not contacting the Court as directed, stated that they are still working on settlement, that they ran into a snag that has to do with repair to one of the buildings and that there still will be a tenants in common ("TIC") agreement, but there are about four roofs that need to be fixed. Ms. Estrem stated that she has nothing to add. Mr. Dobrov stated that they started the TIC process in June and then it stalled, and that they are working on moving forward with having a fourplex distributed out to the Beneficiaries. Status on filing of the Trustee's accounting is continued to January 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to provide a joint status report by January 6, 2020 (one week before the hearing).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Continued To Sep-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kellyann Estrem for Barbara Hill (Petitioner).
Michael Dobrov for Carolyn Hill (Respondent).

Mr. Dobrov stated that they are still in the process of settlement, that they need a little more time (2 months), that they are dividing the properties and that they have to do a tenants in common agreement. Status on filing of Successor Trustee's accounting is continued to September 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to advise the Court as to where settlement stands by September 9, 2019.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins for Barbara Hill.

Mr. Goins stated that the Respondent has a Lawyer, but has not made an appearance because the matter settled, that they are tussling with the settlement agreement, and that they are dealing with tenants in common documents and with the parent child exclusion issue. Status on filing of the Successor Trustee's accounting is continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Continued To May-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins for Petitioner.
Michael Dobrov for Carolyn Hill (Respondent).

Mr. Goins stated that the parties have settled all matters, that they are in the process of finalizing a comprehensive, but complicated settlement, that it involves drafting of a tenants in common agreement and that he will then file a dismissal. Status on filing of the Trustee's accounting is continued to May 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins appeared by telephone for Barbara Hill (Petitioner).
Michael Dobrov for Carolyn Hill (Repsondent).

The Court questions whether Mr. Dobrov is the attorney of record for Carolyn Hill. Mr. Doborv stated that Carolyn's initial attorney was never the attorney of record either, that he filed a substitution of attorney, but it got rejected and that he is representing Carolyn Hill. Mr. Dobrov also stated that he is working with Mr. Goins on the accounting and that the records are insufficient to get it passed by the Probate Examiner. Mr. Goins stated that the matter has been tentatively resolved, and asked for a continuance to finalize the settlement and tenancy in common. Status on filing of the Trustee's accounting is continued to February 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Jan-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-01-2018 Continued To Nov-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins appeared by telephone for (Petitioner).
Michael Dubrov for and with Carolyn Hill (Respondent).
Leon Ainer also present.

Mr. Dubrov stated that the substitution of attorney is in route. Mr. Ainer stated that the accounting is still in draft form and that it was sent over to the new Attorney in draft form. Mr. Dubrov stated that he has not looked at the accounting and that he would like a couple of months. Mr. Goin stated that he has met and conferred with Mr. Dubrov, that he understands and that he is fine with what ever time Mr. Dubrov needs. Status on filing of the Trustee's accounting is continued to November 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects that the accounting be filed by then.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Trustee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Oct-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins for and with Barbara Hill (Beneficiary).
Leon Ainer for Carolyn Hill (Trustee).

Mr. Ainer stated that this accounting spans over four years, that he did not show up at the last hearing because his office did not receive notice, that he and Counsel have met more than twice and that Counsel is aware that they have subpoenaed the bank. Mr. Ainer also stated that they even talked about concluding this matter and distributing what they have as of now pursuant to the terms of the Trust, that if there are any discrepancies they will address them, that they are missing a couple of months which is less than ten percent of all of the items that need to be accounted for and that they are close to getting the accounting. In addition, Mr. Ainer stated that they have to get new appraisals, that they need more information on what has been paid and that they should be fairly close in 60-days. Mr. Goins stated that it is going on two years that they do not have a complete and clear accounting, that they have been waiting since 2004 for the terms of the Trust to be followed, that he would like to get a Court order to remove the respondent and allow his Client to come in as Successor Trustee, that he has not filed a petition and that they need a formal accounting from the time the Decedent passed away. The Court informed Mr. Goins that it will not act on a verbal request, noted that it signed an order on July 19th to file an accounting and ordered Ms. Hill to file an accounting with what they have for the Court to approve it. Status on filing of Trustee's accounting is continued to October 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be filed by September 24, 2018 one week prior to the next hearing date. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Compelling Successor Trustee, Carolyn Hill, To Account
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins appeared by telephone for Barbara Hill.

Mr. Goins stated that he provided a proposed order on Friday, and that he made every effort to resolve this matter, but he was stone walled time and time again. The Court granted the Petition for Order Compelling Successor Trustee Carolyn Hill to Account (subject to review of the order). Status on filing of the accounting is set for September 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will issue an order to appear to Carolyn Hill and her Attorney Leon Ainer to appear on September 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
